mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during 3-month periods of falling FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and negative returns dominate in easing windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above a restrictive threshold, higher short rates increase discount rates and tighten risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which lowers forward earnings expectations and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and falling unemployment jointly indicate strengthening real activity and earnings breadth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in the majority of joint-signal episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which lifts equity earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration triggers are not associated with positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is below 50% across a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening cyclical demand and softer future construction activity, which weighs on risk assets and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive housing-start declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average forward return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising while inflation stays contained, real output growth improves earnings leverage without forcing tighter policy, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO combined with stable CPIAUCSL does not beat either proxy alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the joint high-output/low-inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-rate-hike environment, if CPI remains elevated while housing starts and retail sales weaken, investors should reprice growth and margin risk lower, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation plus weakening-housing-and-sales state does not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most such episodes despite the regime filter.
